title: NDI Reserved Keys (V_gamma)
description: Authoritative list of property names reserved by the NDI/DID schema system in V_gamma. Two lists are provided: `schema_file_keys` for names that appear in a class schema file (e.g. base.json, probe_location.json), and `document_instance_keys` for names that appear in an NDI document instance on disk. Schema authors MUST NOT use any reserved name as their own data field name (i.e., as a `_name` inside a `_fields` entry). This manifest is the discoverable surface for the reserved vocabulary; the meta-schema (did_schema_meta.json) is the enforcement surface. The CURIE registry file (CURIE_lookups_meta.json) has its own separate vocabulary and is out of scope here.

schema_file_keys.name: document_class
schema_file_keys.description: Class-identity header. Sub-keys: class_name, class_version, superclasses. Unprefixed by design, mirroring the V_alpha NDI-matlab layout.

schema_file_keys.name: class_name
schema_file_keys.context: inside document_class and inside document_class.superclasses entries

schema_file_keys.name: _schema
schema_file_keys.description: Path or token-substituted path to the superclass schema file.

schema_file_keys.name: _abstract
schema_file_keys.description: If true, this class cannot be instantiated directly; only concrete subclasses may be.

schema_file_keys.name: _must_refer_to_document_class
schema_file_keys.description: Class-name constraint on the referenced document; empty string means any class.

schema_file_keys.name: _mustBeNonEmpty
schema_file_keys.description: Value must be non-empty (per-type semantics defined in the SPEC).

schema_file_keys.name: _mustBeScalar
schema_file_keys.description: Value must be a single element (not array/matrix).

schema_file_keys.name: _blank_value
schema_file_keys.description: Value carried by a freshly constructed blank document. May fail validation.

schema_file_keys.name: _default_value
schema_file_keys.description: Legitimate default value. Must pass validation.

schema_file_keys.name: _queryable
schema_file_keys.description: Whether the field is indexed for did.query / ndi.query.

schema_file_keys.name: _ontology
schema_file_keys.description: CURIE-based annotation of what the field itself denotes. Null permitted.

schema_file_keys.name: _node
schema_file_keys.description: CURIE identifier (prefix:local) of the ontology term.

schema_file_keys.name: _discriminator
schema_file_keys.description: Names the variant-tagging sub-field for discriminated unions.

document_instance_keys.name: class_name
document_instance_keys.context: inside document_class and inside document_class.superclasses entries

document_instance_keys.name: class_version
document_instance_keys.context: inside document_class and inside document_class.superclasses entries

document_instance_keys.name: superclasses
document_instance_keys.description: Snapshot of the inheritance chain at write time.

document_instance_keys.name: _name
document_instance_keys.description: Role name matching a dependency declaration (after numbered-dependency expansion).

document_instance_keys.name: value
document_instance_keys.description: DID UID of the referenced document.

notes: Property-block keys in a document instance equal the `class_name` of a class in the document's inheritance chain. These are reserved by virtue of being class names; they are not enumerated here because they are not fixed strings.

Sub-field names inside named composite types (e.g. `seconds`, `source_unit`, `node`, `name` inside `duration` / `ontology_term`) are NOT reserved keys in the sense of this manifest. They are data field names fixed by the type's specification.
